feed.summary: Oracle and AMD are collaborating to challenge NVIDIA's dominance in the AI GPU market. Oracle plans to deploy 50,000 AMD Instinct MI450 Series GPUs as part of an "AI Supercluster Revolution." This move is expected to significantly impact the competitive landscape for AI hardware.

feed.title: Intel Unveils ‘Crescent Island’ GPU, Targeting AI Inference Market
feed.summary: Intel has announced "Crescent Island," a new data center GPU with 160GB of memory, specifically designed for AI inference. This launch marks a strategic shift for Intel, focusing on power and cost efficiency by using LPDDR5X memory instead of expensive HBM, aiming to compete in the rapidly expanding AI inference market. The new GPU is a crucial part of CEO Lip-Bu Tan's turnaround strategy for Intel, which has faced significant losses and corporate restructuring.

feed.title: Astera Labs (ALAB) Stock Plummets After AMD-Oracle AI Deal – What Investors Should Know
feed.summary: Astera Labs (ALAB) stock plummeted approximately 19% following AMD's announcement of a 50,000-GPU deployment deal with Oracle. Despite this drop, analysts maintain a bullish outlook on ALAB, citing its strong financial performance in Q2 2025 and leadership in "open AI networking." The article emphasizes Astera's focus on open-standard AI connectivity and broad collaborations within the AI infrastructure ecosystem.

feed.title: Intel expands AI portfolio with Crescent Island GPU - Techzine Global
feed.summary: Intel is expanding its AI accelerator portfolio with the new Crescent Island GPU, aiming to regain market share from competitors like Nvidia and AMD. This GPU utilizes the Xe3P architecture and LPDDR5X memory to balance cost-efficiency and performance, targeting AI applications such as "tokens-as-a-service" and generative AI. Intel plans an annual release cycle for new GPUs, with Crescent Island units expected in mid-2026.

feed.title: Apple Was TSMC’s Biggest Customer In 2024, Accounting For 24% Of Revenue, But It Might Lose That Position Due To Increased HPC Orders In 2025 - Wccftech
feed.summary: Apple was TSMC's largest customer in 2024, contributing 24% of its revenue, but this position is challenged by increasing High Performance Computing (HPC) orders, particularly from NVIDIA. HPC orders accounted for 60% of TSMC's revenue in Q2 2025, and NVIDIA is projected to contribute 19-21% of TSMC's overall revenue in 2025, potentially surpassing Apple. Despite this, Apple has plans to reclaim its top spot in 2026 with multiple 2nm chipsets and continued collaboration on advanced manufacturing.

feed.title: Oracle and AMD expand a partnership with deals in the artificial intelligence sector booming - AccessWdun
feed.summary: Oracle and AMD are expanding their partnership with the deployment of 50,000 AMD graphic processing units for an AI "supercluster" starting in Q3 2026. This collaboration highlights the increasing investment and intertwined deals within the booming AI sector, despite concerns from some analysts about potential tech stock overvaluation.

feed.title: ASML Stock Soars on AI Mega-Deal as Q3 Earnings Loom – Key Facts and Outlook
feed.summary: ASML's stock surged in mid-October 2025, buoyed by a significant AI partnership with Mistral AI and strong Q3 earnings expectations. The company, a linchpin in advanced chip manufacturing, faces geopolitical challenges like export restrictions but continues to benefit from the AI boom and its monopolistic position in lithography. Analysts remain largely bullish, citing strong demand for its EUV systems and a robust order backlog despite cautious 2026 guidance.

feed.title: Inside Intel’s Fab 52: Can America’s most ambitious chip bet pay off?
feed.summary: Intel's Fab 52 in Arizona represents a monumental effort to revive the company's semiconductor dominance and onshore chip manufacturing in the US. This facility is central to Intel's strategy to regain leadership with its new 18A process, powering future processors. The success of Fab 52 is crucial for Intel's comeback and the broader US national security and economic policy in the competitive global chip industry.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley says the colossal AI spending spree could pay for itself by 2028
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley projects that the massive capital expenditure in AI will generate substantial returns, with AI software revenue expected to reach $1.1 trillion by 2028. This outlook suggests that the current AI spending boom is a long-term profit cycle, not a speculative bubble. The bank's analysis aims to reassure investors concerned about the significant investments made by companies like OpenAI, Oracle, Nvidia, and Advanced Micro Devices.

feed.title: Intel To Sample Crescent Island AI Accelerator Next Year - Silicon UK
feed.summary: Intel announced a new data center GPU, Crescent Island, aimed at AI inference workloads, which will begin sampling with customers in the second half of next year. The company is seeking to establish a stronger foothold in the growing AI infrastructure market by focusing on AI inference and agents, as well as a "full-stack AI solution." This move comes as Intel works to turn around its business, including investments from Nvidia and the US government, and its efforts to offer foundry services.

feed.title: Global Markets Weekly Update - T. Rowe Price
feed.summary: This weekly update details global market performance in October 2025, focusing on intensified U.S.-China trade tensions impacting U.S. stocks, rising gold prices, and caution from the Federal Reserve regarding rate cuts. It also covers economic data from Europe, Japan, and China, including Germany's industrial output decline, Japan's political leadership changes, and China's muted Golden Week consumption, alongside central bank rate adjustments in the Philippines and Poland.

feed.title: OpenAI's 'Next Five-Year Plan': New Revenue, New Financing, and New Hardware to Support a Trillion-Dollar Compute Spending Commitment? - 富途牛牛
feed.summary: OpenAI is embarking on an ambitious five-year plan to achieve $1 trillion in expenditures by diversifying revenue streams, innovating financing methods, and deploying new hardware. The company is developing customized AI solutions, commercializing new products like Sora, and collaborating on AI-powered personal assistant hardware. To fund massive infrastructure investments, OpenAI is exploring 'creative' debt financing and stimulating the chip financing market through long-term procurement contracts with major chip suppliers.

feed.title: 1 No-Brainer Artificial Intelligence (AI) ETF to Buy With $65 Ahead of 2026 - Mitrade
feed.summary: The article recommends the Roundhill Generative AI and Technology ETF (CHAT) as a strong investment in the artificial intelligence sector, highlighting its significant outperformance compared to the S&P 500. The ETF, priced under $65 per share, offers exposure to leading AI companies like Nvidia, Alphabet, Microsoft, and Oracle. Despite its higher expense ratio due to active management, the ETF's impressive returns since its inception in May 2023 make it an attractive option for diversifying a portfolio with AI exposure.

feed.title: Intel Re-Enters The AI Chip Race With ‘Crescent Island’ GPU, Aiming To Challenge Nvidia
feed.summary: Intel is re-entering the AI chip market with its new 'Crescent Island' GPU, scheduled for customer sampling in the second half of 2026, aiming to compete with Nvidia and AMD. The company plans an annual release cycle for AI data chips and will focus on AI inference workloads. Notably, Nvidia has invested $5 billion in Intel, taking a 4% ownership stake, indicating a potential collaboration for next-generation chips.

feed.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Stock Drops After Oracle Selects AMD as Chip Partner - Blockonomi
feed.summary: Nvidia (NVDA) stock dropped 4.4% after Oracle announced a partnership with AMD to deploy 50,000 AI processors, raising competition concerns for Nvidia in the AI chip market. Despite this, Nvidia secured new deals, including a $2 billion investment in Elon Musk's xAI and approval to sell up to 500,000 advanced AI chips annually to the UAE, opening new revenue streams. The company also launched the DGX Spark, a compact AI supercomputer, and maintained strong financials with substantial revenue and profit margins.

feed.title: The AI Ouroboros - The American Prospect
feed.summary: This article discusses the "AI Ouroboros" phenomenon, where companies like Nvidia and OpenAI engage in circular financing by investing in startups that then purchase their products. It highlights the financial unsustainability of this model, particularly for OpenAI's ambitious buildout plans, which involve significant deals with Oracle and AMD despite a lack of sufficient cash. The piece warns of a potential AI bubble due to these practices, coupled with looming shortfalls in revenue and power infrastructure.

feed.title: Nvidia Poised to Become TSMC's Largest Customer - The Tradable
feed.summary: Nvidia is on track to surpass Apple as TSMC's largest customer by 2025, driven by the explosive demand for AI processors. This shift highlights a fundamental transformation in the tech industry, with AI becoming the primary growth engine for semiconductor demand. Nvidia's significant control over TSMC's advanced CoWoS packaging capacity underscores its market dominance and TSMC's strategic pivot towards AI.

feed.title: Xbox Next-Gen Console Price Surfaces, You Better Start Saving - GAMINGbible

Meanwhile, Microsoft is launching the ROG Xbox Ally handheld this month, allowing users to play Xbox and PC games on the go.

feed.summary: The 3D IC market is projected to grow from USD 16.85 billion in 2025 to USD 50.19 billion by 2033, driven by a CAGR of 14.64%. North America currently dominates the market, while Asia Pacific is expected to experience the fastest growth.

feed.title: Southern Company price target raised to $87 from $78 at KeyBanc - TipRanks

The firm notes that Southern Company's stock is currently trading at a 12% premium compared to its peers, a premium that KeyBanc believes is justified by the company's strong regulatory environment, particularly in Georgia. However, KeyBanc suggests that the company's growth opportunities may be more limited than the market perceives, primarily due to a recent three-year regulatory "stay-out" and the absence of new rates in its largest jurisdiction, which could hinder its ability to raise its EPS growth rate despite aggressive capital investments in Georgia.

feed.title: Crescent Island: Intel’s bold bet to rival Nvidia in AI chips
feed.summary: Intel has announced its new Crescent Island GPU, a bold move to compete with Nvidia in the AI accelerator market, specifically targeting AI inference. This GPU, built on Intel's Xe3P microarchitecture and featuring 160 GB of LPDDR5X memory, is designed for high performance per watt and efficient real-time inference tasks, signaling Intel's strategic shift towards an air-cooled, open-ecosystem AI platform. The company aims to leverage its strengths in integration and cost-efficiency to challenge Nvidia's dominance, particularly in energy-conscious data centers.

feed.title: OpenAI Plans Five-Year Strategy to Fund Over $1 Trillion in AI Spending - Blockonomi
feed.summary: OpenAI is developing a five-year strategy to fund over $1 trillion in AI spending through new revenue sources, debt partnerships, and additional fundraising rounds. The company currently generates $13 billion in annual recurring revenue, primarily from ChatGPT subscriptions, but reported an $8 billion operating loss in the first half of 2025. OpenAI plans to expand its revenue streams by doubling its paying subscriber rate, exploring government contracts, selling AI computing power through Stargate, and possibly introducing advertisements, in addition to securing over 26 gigawatts of computing capacity from partners like Nvidia and Oracle.
